rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in the correct scope (component type or implementation) based on the requirement context
Use implementation-level bindings for signal routing and assignments in the implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports are placed in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons without explicit data values
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
